access_notes: No external access performed. analysis.py supports DANDI/OpenNeuro discovery when run in an environment with network access and appropriate credentials (if needed).
analysis_checklist: 1) Verify dataset contains unit-resolved spikes and LFP. 2) Inspect optogenetic stimulus channels and remove artifact windows. 3) Run unit classification and QC. 4) Compute binned correlations, cross-correlograms with shift predictors, population coupling, and spectral analyses. 5) Run surrogate jitter tests to control for rate effects. 6) Aggregate across sessions with mixed-effects models when multiple animals/sessions are available.

data_sources: When available: DANDI NWB datasets matching keywords 'optogenetic', 'PV', 'SST', 'inhibition', 'laser'
dataset_manifest.quality_notes: Synthetic data are artifact-free by construction. Real NWB datasets need inspection for optogenetic artifacts, spike sorting quality, and unit stability.
dataset_manifest.sample_description: Synthetic dataset: 40 excitatory and 10 inhibitory units over 60 s (1 kHz resolution). Perturbation epoch at 20-40 s with reduced inhibitory firing simulated.

quality_checks: For synthetic data: verified that excitatory pairwise correlations can be computed and the figure generation pipeline runs. For real datasets, the script includes placeholders for waveform-based QC and spike sorting via SpikeInterface and recommends removing artifact periods around light pulses.
